name: Apple Mail MCP
description: Natural language interface for Apple Mail and Apple Calendar with 41 tools: email search, composition, bulk operations, smart inbox analytics, folder management, taxonomy, archiving, drafting, draft verification, attachments, Mail rules advising, bounded export, security-hardened destructive operations, bounded calendar reads, conflict-checked event creation, availability search, and gated calendar management. Bundled Claude Code workflow skills plus Interactive UI Dashboard.

user_preferences.title: Email Preferences
user_preferences.description: Your personal email preferences that will be provided to the AI assistant when using email tools (e.g., 'Default to BCG account, show max 50 emails, prefer Archive folder')

default_account.title: Default Mail Account
default_account.description: When set, email tools default to this account instead of scanning all configured Mail accounts. Use the exact account name as it appears in Apple Mail (e.g. 'Gmail', 'Work', 'iCloud'). Leave blank to query all accounts by default.

default_signature.title: Default Mail Signature
default_signature.description: Optional Apple Mail signature name to apply by default to compose, reply, and forward drafts (e.g. 'TU'). Leave blank to use Mail's existing behavior.

tools.name: get_inbox_overview
tools.description: Dashboard overview across all accounts: unread counts, mailbox structure with unread indicators, recent-email preview, and AI-driven action suggestions.

tools.name: list_inbox_emails
tools.description: List inbox emails across all accounts or a single account. Defaults to 50 most-recent; a single call scans at most 50 messages. max_emails=0 is rejected with UNBOUNDED_SCAN_REQUIRED (full-mailbox walks are disabled; pass a bounded max_emails or page with offset). Canonical params are max_emails and include_read (legacy aliases `limit` / `unread_only` are accepted with a warning). include_read=False binds a bounded newest-first slice before filtering, so Mail never materializes a large Exchange inbox. Mail calls are serialized, so never issue parallel Mail tool calls; returns {emails, errors, warnings} JSON.

tools.name: get_mailbox_unread_counts
tools.description: Per-mailbox or per-account unread counts. Pass summary_only=True for the fast per-account roll-up.

tools.name: list_accounts
tools.description: List all Mail accounts configured in Apple Mail.

tools.name: list_account_addresses
tools.description: List the sender email addresses (aliases) configured for a given Mail account.

tools.name: list_mailboxes
tools.description: List mailboxes (folders) for one account or all accounts, with optional message counts.

tools.name: move_email
tools.description: Move emails by message_ids (preferred, fast). allow_filter_scan=true plus older_than_days is the only filter escape hatch for date/bulk moves; subject_keyword and sender are schema-compat only and always return TARGET_SELECTOR_DEPRECATED even with allow_filter_scan=true. Nested mailbox paths use '/'. Default max 50; dry_run=true previews ID moves.

tools.name: reply_to_email
tools.description: Reply by message_id (required — discover via search_emails or list_inbox_emails). subject_keyword on this tool is schema-compat only and returns TARGET_SELECTOR_DEPRECATED. Default native_format=true composes in Mail's native reply window (keeps colored quote bar and logo signature); reply_body is typed above the quote and needs Mail window focus plus Accessibility permission for the host, or returns REPLY_WINDOW_FOCUS_FAILED. native_format=false is the windowless object-model fallback (plain-text quote). Default mode saves to Drafts and verifies exact Drafts id when Mail exposes one. mode=open saves first then leaves the compose window for review. Supports DEFAULT_MAIL_SIGNATURE, signature_name, include_signature, reply-all, and CC/BCC. body_html is accepted for backward compatibility but ignored for native replies.

tools.name: compose_email
tools.description: Compose a new standalone email from a specific account. Default mode saves quietly to Drafts; mode=open saves first then leaves the window for review. Does not include original thread context and refuses reply-like Re:/Fwd: drafts unless standalone_confirmed=True. Supports DEFAULT_MAIL_SIGNATURE, signature_name, include_signature, TO/CC/BCC, and optional HTML body.

tools.name: forward_email
tools.description: Forward by message_id (required — discover via search_emails or list_inbox_emails). subject_keyword is schema-compat only and returns TARGET_SELECTOR_DEPRECATED. Default mode saves quietly to Drafts; mode=open saves first then leaves the window for review. Supports DEFAULT_MAIL_SIGNATURE, signature_name, include_signature, optional prepended message, and CC/BCC.

tools.name: create_rich_email_draft
tools.description: Build a standalone rich HTML multipart .eml draft and save it to Drafts by default (closes the compose window). Does not include original thread context and refuses reply-like Re:/Fwd: drafts unless standalone_confirmed=True. Use review_in_mail for saved-open review. Prefer over injecting raw HTML into AppleScript content.

tools.name: manage_drafts
tools.description: Complete draft lifecycle: list, find by thread header, create, send, open, delete. action=list returns Drafts ids; action=find scans bounded newest Drafts headers for In-Reply-To or References; send/open/delete require exact draft_id (draft_subject is schema-compat only and returns TARGET_SELECTOR_DEPRECATED). action=create makes a standalone draft and refuses reply-like Re:/Fwd: drafts unless standalone_confirmed=True. Send action is blocked in --read-only and --draft-safe mode.

tools.name: verify_draft
tools.description: Verify one exact Apple Mail Drafts message id. Returns JSON for recipients, subject, body sentinel match, attachment names and sizes, signature detection above the quote, quoted-original status, In-Reply-To and References headers, and mismatch warnings.

tools.name: verify_drafts
tools.description: Verify multiple exact Apple Mail Drafts message ids and merge the per-draft JSON snapshots. Uses exact draft ids only and reports missing or invalid ids.

tools.name: list_email_attachments
tools.description: List attachments by message_ids (required — discover via search_emails or list_inbox_emails). subject_keyword is schema-compat only and returns TARGET_SELECTOR_DEPRECATED. Capped at max_results (default 50) for fast responses on large inboxes.

tools.name: save_email_attachment
tools.description: Save a specific attachment to disk. Requires message_ids from prior search_emails, list_inbox_emails, or list_email_attachments; use list_email_attachments to pick attachment_index. Validates target path — blocks writes to sensitive directories.

tools.name: search_emails
tools.description: Unified email search across 16 filter parameters: subject, body, sender, date range, attachments, flagged, newsletter detection, threads. Defaults to last 48 hours and the default account (DEFAULT_MAIL_ACCOUNT). recent_days=0 without date_from is rejected with UNBOUNDED_SCAN_REQUIRED; pass recent_days=7 or date_from='YYYY-MM-DD' (full-mailbox sweeps are disabled). all_accounts=True overrides default account. body_text requires allow_body_scan=True or returns BODY_SCAN_DISABLED. A single call scans at most 50 messages (hard ceiling) regardless of window, so it stays fast on large inboxes.

tools.name: get_email_by_id
tools.description: Fetch one exact email by the Apple Mail message id returned from search results. Use after search_emails when you need full body or stable metadata without another broad subject search.

tools.name: get_email_by_ids
tools.description: Fetch multiple exact emails by reviewed Apple Mail message ids, chunked internally to avoid long AppleScript predicates. Returns JSON with items, missing_ids, and invalid_ids.

tools.name: get_email_thread
tools.description: Return the full conversation thread for an email, grouped by subject across Inbox + Sent. Prefer message_id from search_emails or list_inbox_emails; subject_keyword is optional when message_id is set. Defaults to last 48 hours; recent_days=0 is rejected with UNBOUNDED_SCAN_REQUIRED (pass recent_days=7 or smaller; full-mailbox sweeps are disabled).

tools.name: update_email_status
tools.description: Batch mark read/unread, flag/unflag by message_ids (preferred). allow_filter_scan=true plus older_than_days or apply_to_all is the only filter escape hatch; subject_keyword and sender are schema-compat only and always return TARGET_SELECTOR_DEPRECATED. Default max 10 per call.

tools.name: manage_trash
tools.description: Trash lifecycle: move_to_trash, delete_permanent, empty_trash. Prefer message_ids; allow_filter_scan=true plus older_than_days only for date/bulk paths — subject_keyword and sender always return TARGET_SELECTOR_DEPRECATED. empty_trash requires confirm_empty=True. Default max 5.

tools.name: create_mailbox
tools.description: Create a new mailbox (folder) in an account. Supports nested paths and auto-creates parents.

tools.name: synchronize_account
tools.description: Explicitly confirmed Mail.app sync for a single account or all accounts. Requires confirm_sync=True because syncing may fetch a large remote backlog.

tools.name: get_awaiting_reply
tools.description: Sent emails that haven't received a reply. Cross-references Sent vs Inbox over a configurable window (default 7 days).

tools.name: get_needs_response
tools.description: Unread emails that likely need a response. Filters out newsletters/automated senders, assigns priority hints, and excludes already-replied and already-drafted mail by default.

tools.name: get_top_senders
tools.description: Rank senders by frequency over a date window (default 30 days). days_back=0 is rejected with UNBOUNDED_SCAN_REQUIRED (pass days_back=7 or 30; full-mailbox sweeps are disabled). Optional grouping by domain. Top-N sort happens Python-side for fast responses.

tools.name: get_statistics
tools.description: Email analytics: account overview, sender stats, or mailbox breakdown. Short windows fan across 10 mailboxes, longer windows across 20, each capped at 50 messages per mailbox. days_back=0 is rejected with UNBOUNDED_SCAN_REQUIRED (pass days_back=7 or 30; full-mailbox sweeps are disabled) to prevent unbounded scans on large inboxes.

tools.name: export_emails
tools.description: Export emails to txt or html, hard-capped at 50 emails per call. Prefer exact message_ids for reviewed batches (a message_ids list over 50 is rejected before Mail.app runs). single_email requires message_id; filtered, correspondent, and thread scopes discover bounded message ids first; correspondent includes Sent by default for back-and-forth history. entire_mailbox exports a bounded page (default 25) from a mailbox. Unsupported formats, including pdf, fail before Mail.app runs.

tools.name: full_inbox_export
tools.description: Disabled: returns UNBOUNDED_EXPORT_DISABLED. Full-mailbox walks are no longer supported because they overload Mail.app on large inboxes. Use the bounded tools export_emails, list_inbox_emails, or search_emails instead. The tool stays registered so existing configs keep loading.

tools.name: inbox_dashboard
tools.description: Interactive UI dashboard for MCP Apps-compatible clients (requires mcp-ui-server). Account cards, recent emails, and search.

tools.name: list_calendars
tools.description: List every calendar with id, writability, defaults, and engine diagnostics

tools.name: list_events
tools.description: List events in a bounded window with query filter, recurring expansion, and paging

tools.name: get_events_by_id
tools.description: Fetch full detail (notes, alarms, attendees) for exact event ids, window-bounded

tools.name: check_availability
tools.description: Busy blocks and free slots folded in Python over a bounded window (max 62 days)

tools.name: create_event
tools.description: Create one event with timezone-correct times, alarms, recurrence, and conflict detection

tools.name: batch_create_events
tools.description: Create up to 25 one-off events on one calendar with all-or-nothing validation

tools.name: update_event
tools.description: ID-first PATCH update with recurring span rules and attendee-set diffing

tools.name: delete_events
tools.description: Exact-id bulk delete with dry-run default, caps, and whole-series span confirmation

tools.name: manage_calendars
tools.description: Create, rename, or delete calendars; delete is triple-gated with a cascade preview

tools.name: respond_to_invitation
tools.description: Documented refusal: no public macOS API can RSVP to invitations
